p.seg: Alexander by the grace of God king of Scots to the bishops, abbots, earls, barons, justices, sheriffs, provosts, officials, and all worthy men of his whole land, clerk and lay, greeting:

Let present and future know that I have granted, and by this my charter confirmed, to the church of St Mary at Melrose, and to the monks serving God there, the donation that Walter son of Alan made to the same church and monks, of the land of Mauchline, and of pasture in his forest; and of one ploughgate of land towards the land of Douglas, and of a fishery at the mouth of the River Ayr, and of the land of Edmonston, and of twenty acres of land with a toft at Berwick. Also, the whole of the land that John son of Orm gave to them in the territory of Hownam, by those right bounds that are contained in the charter of the aforesaid John that they have of him from the same land. Also, the whole land within the feu of Hownam, from the Capehope Burn upwards by that pathway to the boundary between Raeshaw and Cuthberthope, and from there by the same right bounds that are described in the charter of William the son of John, which they have from him. Also, the donation of the land of Ringwood that Osulf the son of Uhtred, and Uhtred his son, bestowed to them. Also, the donation that Cosptric made the same monks of the land of Hartside, and Spott, and a saltpan. Also, the donation that William de Bruce made to them of a fishery near the church of Rainpatrick, with one acre of land and pasture for four cows, and six oxen, and other easements of the toun of Rainpatrick. Also, two oxgangs of land, and two acres of meadow, with pasture for four hundred ewes that the abbot and convent of Kelso gave to them as a token of the peace made between them. Also, the whole land that they have from Patrick of Ryedale in the territory of Whitton, by those bounds that are contained in the charter of Patrick of Ryedale, which they have from him concerning the same land. Also, the donation that Richard de Morville and Avicia his wife made of the chapel of Park to the same monks, with the land belonging to it; and in addition, of the land of Milkenesside, by its right bounds, which are contained in the charter of the aforesaid Richard and Avicia his wife. Also, the whole land and pasture that William de Morville gave to them to enlarge the aforesaid land of Milkenesside by the right bounds described in Williams’s charter. Also, the whole portion of land in the territory of Kilbewocistun, which Alan son of Roland gave them, by the right bounds described in Alan’s charter, in exchange for the land that William de Morville apportioned to them in Cunningham, with common pasture and other easements of the aforesaid toun, just as it is contained in Alan’s charter. Also, the land that Walter Corbet, son of Walter Corbet, gave them in the territory of Clifton, by the right bounds described in the charter of the same Walter Corbet that they have from him concerning the same land. Also, that half-ploughgate of land, in land and meadow, which Anselm of Whitton gave them from his feu of Mow, by the same rights bounds that are contained in Anslem’s charter that the monks have from him. Also, the donation that Earl Patrick made to them of the whole arable land called Sorrowlessfield beyond Leader from the western side towards the grange of the aforesaid monks, just as William Sorrowless fully held it. Also, a half-ploughgate of the land that Richard of London gave them in Lessudden, just as Richard’s charter to them bears witness. I have also granted to them the quit claim that David Oliphant and Walter his son and heir made to God and the church of Melrose and the aforesaid monks, of Hartside, and of Spott, just as it is contained in the charter of the lord king, William my father. I have also granted the friendly agreement made between Earl Patrick of Dunbar and the aforesaid monks over the controversy that arose between them concerning the pasture to the west of the Leader, just as the writing made on the matter bears witness. I have also granted them the quit claim that Alan son of Walter made to them for the claim there was between them on the pasture on the west side of the Leader. For this reason I will and command that the aforesaid monks shall have and hold all these aforesaid possessions in free and perpetual alms, as freely and quietly, fully and honourably, as the charters of the donors and the confirmations of my predecessors bear witness, saving my service.

As witnesses: William del Bois my chancellor, Saer de Quincy earl of Winchester, Earl Malcolm of Fife, Philip de Valgones my chamberlain, William de Valognes his son, Robert my chaplain, John of Maxwell, David the marischal, Thomas of Lundie my doorward, Hervey the marischal, Jordan Comyn.
At Edinburgh on the third day of April.
